title: SlideML
description: SlideML — typed YAML language that compiles to .pptx. A document declares a deck (palette/fonts/chrome) and a list of slides; each slide picks a layout from the SlideML core registry and fills its slots. Generated from the live layout registry; do not hand-edit.

slideml.description: Schema version. Accept either the number 1 or the string "1".

language.description: BCP-47 language tag. CJK locales (zh-*, ja-*, ko-*) trigger the CJK font fallback.

theme.description: Theme name. The agent reads the named theme's advice (palette, imagery, voice) when authoring; the renderer reads `palette`/`fonts`/`style` blocks below if present, otherwise falls back to the named theme's tokens. Built-in: technical-blue, editorial-warm, midnight-executive, forest-moss, charcoal-minimal. Custom themes can live under ~/.cowork/themes/<name>/.

palette.description: Brand palette — token name → 6-char hex (no #). Required tokens: bg-canvas, bg-card, brand-primary, brand-deep, text-strong, text-muted, accent, divider. Custom token names are allowed but only the standard set is consumed by built-in layouts.

fonts.description: Font family names by role. Each role accepts a single family or an ordered fallback list.

titleAccentRule.description: Whether slideTitle() draws the brand accent rule under titles. Default true.

contrastTarget.description: WCAG contrast enforcement at theme load.

oxml.description: OOXML scheme overrides written to theme1.xml (so PowerPoint's color picker reflects the brand). Values reference token names from `palette`.

chrome.description: Names of chrome modules to enable for every slide unless suppressed by `slide.chrome`.

Slide.description: One slide. `layout` selects the renderer; `slots` carries content.

layout.description: Layout name from the SlideML core registry. The enum is closed by design — themes do NOT add layouts. Extension contract: if you need a layout that doesn't exist here, do NOT invent a name (the renderer will reject unknown layouts). Instead pick the closest existing layout and embed bespoke content via a `component-ref` slot. New layouts are added to the SlideML core after community review, then become available everywhere.

notes.description: Speaker notes for this slide (plain text).

transition.description: Slide transition (currently informational; not emitted to OOXML).

slots.description: Per-layout slot map. Constraints below depend on the chosen `layout`.

title.description: Single-line text, ≤ 30 chars. Supports the SlideML inline-markdown vocabulary (see `InlineMarkdown` $def): **bold**, *italic*, `code`, {up|down|flat|ok|warn|bad|highlight:value} chips, :icon-name:.
Single-line text, ≤ 50 chars. Supports the SlideML inline-markdown vocabulary (see `InlineMarkdown` $def): **bold**, *italic*, `code`, {up|down|flat|ok|warn|bad|highlight:value} chips, :icon-name:.

sub.description: Sub-bullets — one level of nesting only. Each item is a string ≤ itemMaxChars.

title.description: Single-line text, ≤ 50 chars. Supports the SlideML inline-markdown vocabulary (see `InlineMarkdown` $def): **bold**, *italic*, `code`, {up|down|flat|ok|warn|bad|highlight:value} chips, :icon-name:.

takeaway.description: Inline markdown, ≤ 160 chars. See `InlineMarkdown` $def: **bold**, *italic*, `code`, {up|down|flat|ok|warn|bad|highlight:value} chips, :icon-name: (12-icon enum).

title.description: Single-line text, ≤ 60 chars. Supports the SlideML inline-markdown vocabulary (see `InlineMarkdown` $def): **bold**, *italic*, `code`, {up|down|flat|ok|warn|bad|highlight:value} chips, :icon-name:.

code.description: Multi-line text, ≤ 1600 chars. Accepts a string OR an array. Array items are either plain strings (paragraphs) or typed paragraphs `{ kind: "quote" | "note" | "callout" | "h2", text }` for distinctive styling. Each paragraph supports SlideML inline markdown (see `InlineMarkdown` $def).

density.description: One of: loose | normal | dense | micro.

BandSpec.description: Header / footer band content. A bare string is shorthand for `{ left }`.

BackgroundSpec.description: Slide background. Either a solid color or an embedded image.

src.description: Path, http(s) URL, or data: URL.

ChromeSpec.description: Chrome control. "default" applies all enabled chrome modules; "none" suppresses them all; the object form mixes four mechanisms: legacy boolean flags (header/footer/brandBar/pageNumber), `enable: [...]` to add modules the theme didn't pre-declare, `disable: [...]` to suppress modules for this slide, and `override: { module: { ... } }` for per-module parameter overrides (e.g. `override: { 'page-footer': { left: 'CONFIDENTIAL' }, 'brand-bar': { color: 'accent' } }`).

enable.description: Chrome modules to ADD on top of the theme's declared list.

disable.description: Chrome modules to suppress for this slide.

override.description: Per-module parameter overrides. Keys are chrome module names; values are module-specific. Examples: page-footer: { left, center, right }; brand-bar: { color, height }; watermark: { text, color, alpha, position }; progress-bar: { color, track, height }; section-marker: { color, prefix }; hairline: { color, position, weight, insetCm }.

ImageRef.description: Image reference. CANONICAL form: `{ src, alt?, aspectRatio?, fit?, shape?, border?, overlay?, cornerRadius? }`. Two alternate forms are accepted for ergonomics: a bare path/URL string (auto-wrapped to `{ src }`), and `{ url, ... }` where `url` is a deprecated alias for `src` (kept for compatibility). Prefer `src` in new content.

oneOf.description: Bare path / URL — auto-wrapped to { src }.

src.description: Canonical. Path, http(s) URL, or data: URL.

svg.description: Inline SVG markup (the literal `<svg ...>...</svg>` string). The renderer wraps it as a `data:image/svg+xml` URL automatically. Use this when an agent generates an icon, sparkline, or diagram on the fly without writing it to disk first.

fit.description: How the image fits its bounding box. Default `cover`.

aspectRatio.description: Optional intrinsic aspect ratio (width / height) of the source image. Lets the renderer reserve space accurately and lets image_gen size the output to match. Common values: 1.778 (16:9), 1.0 (square), 0.75 (3:4 portrait).

shape.description: Clip silhouette. Default 'square' (no clipping). 'circle' is great for headshots.

cornerRadius.description: Corner radius for shape='rounded'. Fraction of the shorter side.

width.description: Border width in EMU (use ctx.pt(N) when authoring).

alpha.description: 0 = invisible, 1 = opaque. ~0.4 makes for a tasteful tint.

overlay.description: Translucent colored fill drawn on top — handy for hero images that need text contrast.

crop.description: Inset crop fractions (0..1 per side). 0.1 left = trim left 10%.

softEdge.description: Feathered edge — fade the image into the canvas. 0..0.5 fraction of the shorter side. 0.05–0.15 reads as 'editorial soft', 0.3+ becomes a vignette-like fade.

shadow.description: Drop shadow under the image (OOXML outerShdw).

blur.description: Blur radius in EMU. Use ctx.pt(N) when authoring.
Gaussian blur radius (EMU). Use for hero backgrounds.

duotone.description: Two-tone recolour. Pair with brand colours for editorial / magazine look.

ChartSpec.description: Chart specification. CROSS-FIELD CONSTRAINT (enforced by the SlideML compiler, not by JSON Schema): every series's `values` array MUST have the same length as `data.labels` (except scatter charts, which use `points: [{x,y}]` instead). Pie / doughnut charts use only the first series. Combo charts pick a per-series `type: "bar" | "line"`. Waterfall charts treat any series value of `null` (or non-finite) as a 'total' bar — surrounding values are deltas applied to a running total.

values.description: Length MUST equal data.labels.length (compiler-enforced). Required for every chart type except scatter (which uses `points`). Waterfall: a `null` element marks a 'total' bar.

type.description: Per-series chart type — only valid when parent chart `type` is "combo".

points.description: Scatter only — list of {x,y} pairs, replaces `values`.

format.description: Y-axis number format. MUST be an object — bare strings are rejected.

annotations.description: Inline highlights drawn over the chart by the layout. Each annotation is a callout/marker/band anchored at a category index (`at`) or range (`[start, end]`).

label.description: Label text — supports SlideML inline markdown.

at.description: Category index this annotation refers to.

range.description: Inclusive [start, end] category range — used for band-style annotations.

TableCell.description: Table cell — either a bare string/number, or an object with optional emphasis (chip-coloured + bold).

emphasis.description: Tints the cell using the same chip colour palette as inline `{...}` chips.

align.description: Per-column horizontal alignment. Index N applies to column N. When omitted, numeric-looking strings auto-right-align and everything else left-aligns.

Region.description: Polymorphic dashboard cell. `kind` picks the shape: kpi | chart | table | text | bullets | image | code | quote | sparkline | progress.

body.description: Text body. Accepts a string OR a string[] joined with paragraph breaks.

area.description: Fill below the line. Default false.

InlineMarkdown.description:
SlideML inline-markdown vocabulary (used by every text/text-block/markdown-inline slot, every bullet, every region title/body, every table cell). Anything outside this vocabulary renders as plain text.

  Construct           | Example                  | Renders as
  --------------------|--------------------------|----------------------------------------
  **bold**            | **critical**             | bold run
  *italic*            | *aside*                  | italic run
  `code`              | `compile()`              | mono run
  {up:value}          | {up:+12% YoY}            | ▲ +12% YoY in semantic-up colour
  {down:value}        | {down:-3pp}              | ▼ -3pp in semantic-down colour
  {flat:value}        | {flat:—}                 | → — in muted colour
  {ok:value}          | {ok:done}                | ✓ done
  {warn:value}        | {warn:risk}              | ⚠ risk
  {bad:value}         | {bad:miss}               | ✗ miss
  {highlight:value}   | {highlight:critical}     | ● critical in accent colour
  :icon-name:         | :check: :warning:        | unicode glyph from the 12-icon enum

Icon enum: check, x, star, arrow-up, arrow-down, dot, warning, info, clock, users, chart, code.
